title: 2 Boston men accused of assaulting man who hit their car while trying to park
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Anthony Shearrion, 41, and Arkie Swinson, 45, are accused of assault after a parking incident in Dorchester.
Police say the suspects followed the victim who hit their car, then allegedly smashed his window and beat him.
The suspects, held at Suffolk County Jail after arraignment, pleaded not guilty and are due back in court April 14.

title: 2026 Red Sox preview: 4 questions facing Alex Cora’s club
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony is expected to lead the Boston Red Sox offense in the 2026 season.
The Red Sox struggled with power hitting last season, ranking 15th in home runs with 186.
Boston must address its infield defense, having led the majors with 116 errors last season.

title: Healey announces kickoff of public safety initiative for 2026 World Cup
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey announced 'Match-Ready Massachusetts', a public safety initiative for the 2026 World Cup in Foxborough.
Massachusetts secured $76 million in federal funding for World Cup security, focusing on cybersecurity, emergency preparedness, and public health.
State will offer special transportation arrangements, including event trains, for the seven World Cup matches between June 13 and July 9.

title: Kraft-owned New England Revolution backing White Stadium renovation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The New England Revolution supports the White Stadium renovation in Franklin Park, which faces a legal challenge.
Boston Legacy FC and public-private funding are driving the White Stadium project, facing opposition over parkland use.
Estimated costs for taxpayers and community benefits agreements for White Stadium renovation reach $387 million total.

title: Maine man in critical condition after stopping to help stranded driver
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 54-year-old Maine man is in critical condition after being hit on I-95 in Veazie while helping a stranded driver.
A car driven by a 19-year-old woman lost control in snow and struck the man and an 18-year-old woman.
Police say the man may have shielded the woman during the crash; all involved were taken to the hospital.

title: Berkshire County man arrested after suspicious early-morning fire in his home
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: State Police arrested John Rohane, 60, after a suspicious fire at his home in Monterey, Massachusetts.
The fire at 340 Main Road was reported around 3:50 a.m.; officials suspect it was intentionally set.
Rohane was found in a nearby wooded area and was hospitalized for issues unrelated to the fire or arrest.

title: Lured by pepperoni, dog rescued from 12-foot backyard sinkhole on Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Tessa, an 11-year-old yellow Labrador, was rescued from a 12-foot sinkhole in her yard in Falmouth, Cape Cod.
Fire and rescue crews used pepperoni to lure Tessa and a pole leash to safely pull her from the sinkhole.
The sinkhole is believed to have formed due to a leak in an underground pipe system, washing away the soil.

title: New England-based rescue takes in batch of neglected axolotls
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Libertyland Axolotl Rescue took in 16 neglected axolotls from an overcrowded situation, seeking donations for their rehabilitation.
The axolotls, named after cheeses, have missing limbs and infections; some already found homes, including Burrata in Florida.
The nonprofit focuses on education, rehabilitation, and adoption, managing over 100 axolotls in 2025 with experienced foster support.

title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein's Love Letters explores how imperfections in objects can symbolize relationships and personal growth.
A24 is hosting a free preview of 'The Drama' starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson in Somerville on Sunday.
Podcast guest Yowei Shaw shares how a burnt table became a reminder of lessons learned in her marriage.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School opened a 25-station cosmetology salon for student training in hair, skin, and nail care.
The salon allows students to gain hands-on experience with public clients, essential for professional licensure.
Students complete 1,000 training hours and may take the state exam for cosmetology licensure post-graduation.
